title: Vanilla Extreme
description:
A vanilla-ish modpack for technical players.
Has OP mods only at very end game to make it feel more vanilla like. Have fun! :D
body:
# **Welcome to Vanilla Extreme!** ## WARNING: Please add Gobber to the pack, you can get it from here: https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/gobber-fabric/files/4086199 ## ***Please read [changes](#keybind-changes) for keybind changes, theres a lot!*** ### *Also check out the changes/additons made with kubejs!* [#KubeJS Additions](#kubejs) --- This pack was made to make a few fun challenges to the normal vanilla flow while being setup for technical players. With a few craftable items for end-game to take control of the world via carpet mod & kubejs! [Mod List](https://modrinth.com/modpack/vanilla-extreme/version/1.0.0) I would like to point out, this pack has the [Gobber](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/gobber-fabric) mod, it adds quite a lot of Overpowered Items and tiers of materials. (Removed the generation of the ore blocks). But rest assured, I made getting these grindy enough to make it feel balanced.
How? You may ask?
Well.....**WARDENS** :D
It has overworld, nether, and end tiers.
So, to get the materials you kill wardens in the various dimensions!

And for the cheaty end-game carpet items, each item will need 36 warden kills in the END.
So it doesnt really matter, you dont HAVE to make them and since it's endgame i dont see a reason to force the vanillaness to maintain, thats mostly when this pack takes over :)
I have decided I reconfigured enough keybinds for every new pack I play to do some of it for you! But I have attempted to do so in a way that wouldnt feel alien
*(you dont move foward with right alt for instance XD)*

Keybind Changes

Controls But I have also configured various things in the mods such as keyminds for [Masady's mods](https://legacy.curseforge.com/members/masady/projects) and Charm enabling and disabling various things to fit the pack's vanilla-ness. CONFIG:
**Tweakeroo**: `Numpad 7`
**Minihud**: `Numpad 8`
**Litematica**: `Numpad 9`

**Litematica *Menu***: `Numpad +`
# Tweakeroo:
Tweakeroo Config Changes ## Tweakeroo's Tweak Changes:
Tweaks **Accurate Block Placement**: `L_CTRL + F`
> Block Placement Into: I
Block Placement Reverse: O **Breaking Restriction**: `R_ALT + Left Click`
> Column: `X + 1`
Diagonal: `X + 2`
Face: `X + 3`
Layer: `X + 4`
Line: `X + 5`
Plane: `X + 6` **Fake Sneaking**: `L_CTRL + L_SHIFT`
**Fake Sneak Placement**: `L_CTRL + L_SHIFT + Right Click`
**Fast Block Placement**: `L_ALT + Right Click`
**Fast Left Click**: `L_CTRL + Left Click`
**Fast Right Click**: `L_CTRL + Right Click`
**Flexible Block Placement**: `L_ALT + F`
> Adjacent: `Z`
Offset: `X`
Rotation: `C` **Free Camera**: `BACKSPACE`
**Gamma Override**: `G`
> Value modified in `Generic` to `4.0`
This was you can tell whats bright and whats dark. **Hold Attack**: `L_ALT + L_SHIFT + Left Click`
**Hold Use**: `L_ALT + L_SHIFT + Right Click`
**Hotbar Randomizer**: `H + R`
**Repair Mode**: `L_ALT + M`
## Tweakeroo Yeet Changes
Yeets *disable* **Axe Stripping**: `Numpad 1`
*disable* **Shovel Pathing**: `Numpad 2`
*disable* **Entity Rendering**: `L_ALT + E`
*disable* **Nether Fog**: `L_ALT + N`
*disable* **Off Hand Rendering**: `O + F`
*disable* **Rendering Scaffolding**: `L_ALT + S`
## Things ON by default:
Enabled Default **TWEAKS**:
creative Extra Items
Empty Shulker Boxes Stack
Inventory Preview
> Preview: `L_ALT` Lava Visibility
Map Preview
Swap Almost Broken Tools
**YEETS**:
*disable* **Bat Spawning**
*disable* **Double Tap Sprint**
*disable* **Boss Fog**
*disable* **Creative Menu Infested Blocks**
*disable* **Dead Mob Rending**
*disable* **Dead Mob Targeting**
*disable* **Item Switch Render Cooldown**
*disable* **Nether Portal Sound**
*disable* **Portal Gui Closing**
*disable* **Shulker Box Tooltip**
*disable* **Wall Unsprint**
# Minihud's Changes:
Minihud Config Changes **Light Overlay**: `F7`
>Safe areas are not overlayed
**WARNING**: Pillagers can still spawn at level 9 **Overlay Spawn Chunks *REAL***: `Numpad 0` ## Info Lines:
Info Lines *info* **BlockPosition**
*info* **BlockProperties**
*info* **HoneyLevel**
*info* **HorseJump**, **HorseSpeed**
*info* **LookingAtEntity**
--- ***More detailed info***:
*info* **Coordinates**: `R_ALT`
*info* **Entities**: `R_ALT`
*info* **FPS**: `R_ALT`
*info* **LookingAtBlock**: `R_ALT`
*info* **RotationPitch**: `R_ALT`
*info* **RotationYaw**: `R_ALT`
*info* **SlimeChunk**: `R_ALT`
*info* **Speed**: `R_ALT`
*info* **TimeFormatted**: `R_ALT`
# Charm and Cobblegen:
Charm and Cobblegen changes ## Charm: ### Keybinds: Rebounded portable crafting table to `c` ### Disabled: Disabled AzaleaWood
Disabled Bee Keepers
Disabled Chicken dropping feathers
Disabled villager extra trades
Disabled wandering trader extra trades
Disabled Lumberjacks
Disabled Map Preview
> MiniHud and Tweakeroo already do this Disabled Mooblooms
Disabled Quick Replant() > [Harvest with ease](https://modrinth.com/mod/harvest-with-ease) mod does this better. Disabled elytra repair with leather
Disabled netherite scrap repair
Disabled prismarine shards repair trident
Disabled LavaBucketDestroysItems
Disabled Bat Buckets
Disabled stacking books
Disabled witches dropping luck
Disabled Totem of Preserving Grave mode > Only `Ravager's` drop these totems ### Enabled: Enabled End respawn anchors --- ## Cobblegen This mod allows me to add custom generators for various blocks. **Cobbled Deepslate**: cobblestone generators produce Cobbled Deepslate under y0
**Tuff**: Stone generators under y0 produce Deepslate and Tuff.
Chances: Deepslate 60%, Tuff 40% **End Stone**: Endstone can be made with cobblestone generation in the END dimension.
**Geode Generator**: You can have budding amethyst underneath stone generators to produce blocks from a geode, including Budding Amethyst itself! :D
Chances: Calcite 90%, Amethyst block and Budding Amethyst are both 5% Basalt generators:
**Soul Soil**: Basalt (like normal)
**Soul Sand**: Blackstone
**Netherrack**: Netherrack
--- # Generation
Generation Info So, i removed as much generation from most the mods as i could. So if you remove a mod, only thing effected is items in chests. Tho theres some mods contributing to world generation. Twigs: >Twigs adds a few stone based blocks to the overworld and nether. Yung's Better Dungeons
Yung's Better Mineshafts
Yung's Better Ocean Monuments > These mods adds various improvements of structures, but edited the config for not completely replacing them, so its 50/50 on it being Yung's that spawn, or Vanilla's

Also note, they use vanilla blocks so if you remove the mods they wont make bad holes where pregenerated one's *used* to be :)
So these are somewhat less destructive if removed compared to twigs.
Tho idk how bounding boxes would apply, for ocean monuments for example, be carefull with this! :D

KubeJS

KubeJS Modifications **Please note**:
Feel free to edit these script or remove them in any way you want.
But make sure we might not be able to solve bugs that may arise from your doing this, so be careful! ## **Block Modifications**:
Haste Effected Blocks: Modified breaking spead of some blocks, you might LOVE this :D ### Nature Blocks: **Deepslate** (and it's varients)
**Cobblestone**
**End Stone**
**All Vanilla Ores**(*except ancient debris*)
**Glowstone**
### Beacon Blocks: **Gold**
**Iron**
**Emerald**
**Diamond**
**Netherite**
(Charm Waxed Iron and Gobber's blocks might not work) Are now ***ALL*** instamine-able.
Yw :)
--- ## **Added Features**:
New features added 50% of Pigmen killed by charged creepers will convert to piglins.
Making for a unique and ***VERY*** challenging superflat challenge ;)
Entity falling in void in the END Dimension only will be teleport to the overworld as if you fell into the atmosphere, set on fire of course.
**ITEMS AND NON CREATURE ENTITIES SUCH AS BOATS WONT WORK**
Various commands from carpet had been enabled by default when a new world is joined.
Also a spawn radius command is ran to get `spawnRadius` to 0.
So if you need to find spawn points you can die, and spot the block you spawn on if you dont feel like using MiniHud's spawn chunk overlay.
--- ## **Custom Recipes**:
Recipes Recipies has been added for various blocks/items: ### **Dye Recipies**: wool concrete concrete_powder terracotta glass red_sand #### **Undye**: terracotta glass --- ### **Miscellaneous**: 2 Logs to 16 sticks
Easier Dispenser Recipie
Glass back to sand (8 to 4)
cobbled varient from Deepslate and Stone --- ### **Slabs**: From the KubeJS Discord `Lexxie Raven Black` created this script.
It converts slabs back to full blocks.
Works for all vanilla blocks, blocks from `Charm` and `Twigs`
However not with `More Slabs, Stairs, and Walls` oddly, so beware.
--- ## **Custom Items & Blocks**:
Items & Blocks created These items mostly are for the carpet mod and used to access carpet commands through survival. Yes, they are ***VERY*** overpowered, but the recipies has been made for very end-game.
So its up to the player who's 99% likely already beat the game to decide *if* they want to use these or not. Mostly added for technical players to be honest. --- ## **Blocks Created**: I have added a few various building blocks.
No recipies have been made as of yet
# **Experiencing Too much Lag?**:
Lag reduction tips This pack is meant to be somewhat small so it shouldn't lag to much at all.
But incase it is feel perfectly free to remove mods.
Its made in a way to not be impacted too much but make sure you keep track a dependancy and a mod, as if you remove a dependancy it may break a mod that uses it! So pretty much feel free to edit the pack how ever you want :D And if you plan to remove a lot just make sure to keep these for performance: [**Enhanced Block Entities**](https://modrinth.com/mod/ebe)
[**Lithium**](https://modrinth.com/mod/lithium)
[**Smooth Boot**](https://modrinth.com/mod/smoothboot-fabric)(optional tbh, makes loading smoother)
[**Sodium**](https://modrinth.com/mod/sodium)
[**Spark**](https://modrinth.com/mod/spark)(optional, keeps track of what may be causing lag)
[**Starlight**](https://modrinth.com/mod/starlight)
[***Tweakeroo***](#) > This is an odd one, ik, but along with all the many features it adds it also has the disable for entity rendering which can cause a absolutely **MASSIVE** improvement when near farms with many entities. And for the pack's sake i recommend you keep Gobber and Carpet with KubeJS, for the functionality they have together. But if you DO remove KubeJS make sure you remove the `kubejs` folder from your .minecraft, it's separate from the mod's folder. ## **Mods not needed for gameplay**:
Mods which dont effect gameplay You can remove these perfectly finely without any effect on the world. Most which will happen is some features removed, nothing more :)
